action.description: fill: fill a proof operation into a blank; insert_before: insert a step before the target step; amend: modify the arguments of the target step

anyOf.title: Obvious
anyOf.description: the goal holds trivially. Caution: this operation barely unfolds definitions unless you supply them in `facts`. This operation is SLOW!

facts.description: helpful facts for proving the goal

name.description: the exact name of the fact in Isabelle/HOL

instantiations.description: optional instantiations for ∀-quantified or schematic variables

value.description: an Isabelle term to substitute, with all literal numbers annotated with their types

discharge_premises.description: optional list of facts to discharge the premises of this rule left-to-right; use null to skip a premise position

flip.description: if true, reverses an equation (swaps LHS and RHS) so the simplifier rewrites in the opposite direction

proposition.description: an Isabelle proposition with all literal numbers annotated with their types, to be proven in time

anyOf.title: Function
anyOf.description: define a (recursive) function

metric.description: metric for proving recursion termination, one or more Isabelle terms of type `arg1's type × ... × argN's type → nat` demonstrating the recursion is strictly decreasing. Leave it empty to let Isabelle infer, which often works.

proof_operations.description: ordered list of proof operations to apply at target_step. For action 'fill', the first operation fills the blank slot and subsequent operations append as successive siblings. For 'insert_before', operations are inserted contiguously before target_step in order. For 'amend' with a single operation, it replaces target_step (preserving its sub-steps via inheritance); with multiple operations, target_step is deleted and the list is inserted at that position.
